delta.name: Delta
delta.title: Argent Squad Sniper & Infiltration Expert
delta.description: A sharp-tongued Argent Squad sniper and infiltration specialist who learned to survive on the streets of Nar Shaddaa.
delta.voiceStyle: Casual, mocking, New-Yorker edge. Sounds like she’s joking until it’s too late.
delta.tooltip: Delta reflects your character through lethal subtlety and street-hardened instinct

levelGreetings.1: You wanna be a shadow? Try not to trip over your own boots.
levelGreetings.2: First clean hit. Not bad. Still sloppy, but not bad.
levelGreetings.3: Quick in, quick out. Almost impressive.
levelGreetings.4: Now you’re movin’ like a ghost. Don’t chew your luck.
levelGreetings.5: Halfway there. Technique’s solid. Don’t get soft.
levelGreetings.7: Perfect hit’s the one nobody knows happened.
levelGreetings.8: They’re whisperin’ your name now. That’s good.
levelGreetings.10: You’re officially scary. Don’t screw it up.

levelGreetings.1: You wanna sneak? Try not to embarrass us both.
levelGreetings.2: Didn’t set anything on fire. I’m shocked.
levelGreetings.3: Slidin’ through security like you belong.
levelGreetings.4: Cleaner than half my old squad.
levelGreetings.5: Walkin’ into restricted zones like you own ’em. Bold. Dumb. I like it.
levelGreetings.6: Smooth entry, clean exit. Rare combo.
levelGreetings.8: High society to gutter scum without missin’ a beat.
levelGreetings.9: Stop makin’ this look easy. You’re givin’ me expectations.
levelGreetings.10: Anyone. Anywhere. Try not to believe the hype.

assassin_sniper.confirmation: Patience and precision make you untouchable.

assassin_shadow.greeting: Shadow Operative. You exist between moments.

assassin_spy.greeting: Assassin-Spy. You study before you strike.

infiltrator_ghost.greeting: Slippin’ through where you shouldn’t be.

infiltrator_phantom.greeting: They argue if you were real.

default: Workbench is hot, tools are clean, and nobody's yellin' yet. Pick a piece and we'll make it earn its keep.
You bring me the kit, I bring the bad ideas that somehow pass inspection. Let's see what we're fixin'.
empty: Can't tune air, genius. Get a weapon, armor plate, or a piece of gear in your inventory, then we'll make somethin' useful outta it.
Bench is ready, shelves are empty. Real tragic. Bring me hardware and I'll stop starin' at you like this.

default: Weapon on the bench. Good. First rule: don't bolt on every shiny part just because it winks at ya. Slots are rent, and rent is expensive.
A weapon's gotta match the hand holdin' it. We tune the frame, check the balance, then make sure it doesn't betray you when things get loud.
blaster: Blaster frame, huh? Bolt package, heat bleed, trigger feel. Keep it tight or you'll cook the shot before it leaves the barrel.
Blasters are all attitude until the power cell hiccups. We tune the guts first, style second. Usually.
pistol: Pistol work is all draw, snap, and not snaggin' on your coat. Little frame, little tolerance for dumb choices.
Compact blaster, close problems. We keep it quick, clean, and rude.
rifle: Rifle's a patient weapon. Optics, stock, barrel harmonics—yeah, I know big words. Don't look so surprised.
Long gun on the table. Give it stability and it gives you answers from way over there.
carbine: Carbine wants to do two jobs and complain about neither. We keep it light enough to move and mean enough to matter.
Short barrel, fast corners. Don't overbuild it or it'll handle like a drunk bantha.
heavy: Heavy weapon. Subtle as a rancor in a lift. Reinforce the frame before you ask it to make thunder.
Big hardware means big recoil, big heat, big bills. I love it already.
melee: Melee work is balance and grip. If your wrist hates you after one swing, the weapon's tellin' on us.
Edge, haft, weight, follow-through. Close-combat gear rewards restraint, which is annoyin' but true.
vibro: Vibroblade on the slab. Edge frequency's the song; grip and balance are the dance. Don't make either one ugly.
Vibro work is delicate until it ain't. Tune the emitter wrong and the blade screams louder than the target.
grenade: Explosives are not a personality, but sure, let's pretend. Fuse discipline, casing integrity, then we keep our eyebrows.
Thrown ordnance? Keep it reliable. If it gets clever after it leaves your hand, that's how stories end.

default: Armor's a deal with your future bruises. Plate here, flex there, don't seal the joints like you're tryin' to become furniture.
Armor ain't just protection. It's heat, weight, noise, and whether you can still climb a ladder without beggin' for mercy.
light: Light armor's gotta move like clothing and lie like armor. Thin plates, smart padding, no drama.
Light kit means you trust your feet. Fine by me. We keep the protection where the pain likes to land.
medium: Medium armor's the compromise nobody admits they love. Enough plate to matter, enough flex to keep you useful.
Medium kit needs clean joint work. Otherwise you get protected from blaster fire and defeated by stairs.
heavy: Heavy armor walks into a room before you do. Make the seals strong, vents clean, and the weight worth carryin'.
Heavy plate is a promise: you're gonna get hit and keep movin'. Let's make sure the armor keeps its end.
powered: Powered armor? Now we're talkin'. Servos, seals, power draw. Treat it like a machine or it'll treat you like cargo.
Exosystems need respect. One lazy calibration and suddenly your suit thinks kneecaps bend sideways.
bodysuit: Bodysuit work is all comfort until it saves your hide. Stitch the layers right and nobody knows why you're still standin'.
Soft armor, sneaky armor. Keep the seams quiet and the fit mean.

default: Gear is where clever people become problems. Pick the mod that solves the job, not the one that makes the bench look fancy.
Utility kit, my favorite kind of trouble. Small changes, big consequences, and somehow always missing one screw.
tool: Tools should feel like an extra hand, not a committee meeting. We tune access, grip, and not droppin' it into a reactor shaft.
Tool kit on the bench. Make it reliable first. Fancy comes after it stops embarrassin' you.
medical: Medical gear gets no jokes from me. Fast access, clean storage, clear readouts. Panic hates good layout.
Med kit work is about seconds. If you gotta search for the right vial, the kit already failed.
stealth: Stealth gear should disappear before you do. Quiet fasteners, dull finish, nothing janglin' like a tourist.
Sneaky kit, finally. We cut shine, cut noise, and cut the odds of you makin' me deny I know you.
communications: Comms gear needs range, clarity, and fewer mystery squeals. If it sounds haunted, I'm billing extra.
Signal kit on the slab. Boost it wrong and every security tech in three sectors hears you breathe.
sensor: Sensor package? Good. Eyes first, assumptions second. Calibrate it right and it catches lies before you do.
Scanners love clean power and hate impatience. Lucky for them, I got one of those two things.
survival: Survival gear's gotta work when everything else is on fire, frozen, flooded, or actively tryin' to eat you.
Field kit rule: if it needs perfect conditions, it's decoration. We build for ugly.
computer: Computer gear, slicer toys, little blinking headaches. Secure the ports, harden the casing, don't brag in the access logs.
Data kit on the table. Fast is nice. Quiet is better. Not getting traced is what we call art.

guidance.class: Pick the stuff that makes you faster, quieter, deadlier. You wanna sneak past these losers and still be breathin' when the credits hit the floor.
guidance.talents: Go for silent takedowns, smooth escapes, and anything that keeps you from gettin' splattered. Dead men don't talk, but smart ones survive.
guidance.abilities: Dexterity, precision, brains. Strength? Only helps if you ain't gettin' seen. Keep your head down and your eyes open.
guidance.skills: Stealth, anatomy, infiltration—know where to poke so they never get up. Learn it all, kid. Nar Shaddaa ain't forgiving.
guidance.multiclass: Extra skills? Fine. The more ways you can ruin somebody's day, the less likely it is you end up dead in a gutter.
guidance.hp: Toughness helps, but lemme tell ya—if they see you, it's already too late. Move smart, hit fast, survive.
guidance.species: Your species shapes your potential. Choose what suits your path.
guidance.background: Your past defines you. Use it wisely.
guidance.forcePowers: Choose powers that strengthen your purpose.

tier1: Miss once, you don’t get a second shot.
